summary: Fix 3 analysis bugs: scipy NaN in power_analysis, missing MCP tool prefix in normalize_tool_name, misleading per-scenario column label
context: During accuracy review of pooled analysis, found: (1) power_analysis returned NaN for certain df/ncp combos causing MDES=3.24 at n=32 instead of 0.71, misclassifying full-twining as 'inconclusive' despite 95.6% power; (2) normalize_tool_name didn't strip 'mcp__twining__twining_' prefix, causing all 32 Twining tools to show as 'never called'; (3) per-scenario table showed 'spread' (best-worst range) in a column labeled 'Mean'.
rationale: Fix 1: Handle NaN from scipy nct.cdf by treating as 0 (left tail is negligible for large ncp). Fix 2: Add 'mcp__twining__twining_' to _PREFIXES list. Fix 3: Rename column to 'Spread' and add Best/Worst Mean columns.

alternatives.option: Cap ncp at a threshold instead of handling NaN
alternatives.reason_rejected: The NaN is in scipy's internal computation — capping ncp wouldn't fix it for all edge cases
